title: Buildah kills container although SIGTERM signal is handled
body:
**Description**
I run an OCI image created by Buildah as a systemd service with Podman. In the journalctl, there are several logs that seem to belong to a layer added on top to the image by Buildah (they include the Buildah version at the end of the line; I additionally did not notice these logs before using images made with Buildah).

Example:
```
2023-04-22 11:09:45.957612603 +0200 CEST m=+0.931930318 container stop 7fe468112f2cd80a5f06ee6d5daea948975dfad30aaa0e16cfcfa4b99de2e13e (image=ghcr.io/USER/PRIVATE-REPO:dev, name=api, PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=api_container.service, io.buildah.version=1.23.1)
```

_This is why I believe this issue belongs to the issue tracker of Buildah. Feel free to point me to a different repository if I was wrong._

I feel like that kind of "runner" kills my container when stopping the systemd service although the `SIGTERM` process signal is handled by the container which should therefore be stopped gracefully.
I am running a Node.JS server. According to the logs, the server process has the **PID 1** (the line with a JSON-formatted log comes from my project) meaning that it will receive the process signals for stopping the container. This is the code I use to handle `SIGTERM` (you can double-check that, what is important is that the process is exitted in the handler):
```ts
async function shutdown() {
  fastify.log.info("Shutting down server and database pool...");
  await fastify.close();
  await prisma.$disconnect();
  process.exit(0);
}

process.on("SIGTERM", shutdown);
```


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
https://github.com/MatsG23/buildah-issue


**Describe the results you received:**
The container seems to be killed. It says "Container died" which sounds like a non-graceful stop to me.

Logs:
```
Apr 22 11:09:44 api-sys systemd[524]: Stopping API Server (Podman Container)...
Apr 22 11:09:45 api-sys podman[6532]: {"level":30,"time":1682154585423,"pid":1,"hostname":"7fe468112f2c","msg":"Shutting down server and database pool..."}
Apr 22 11:09:45 api-sys podman[19623]: 2023-04-22 11:09:45.957612603 +0200 CEST m=+0.931930318 container stop 7fe468112f2cd80a5f06ee6d5daea948975dfad30aaa0e16cfcfa4b99de2e13e (image=ghcr.io/USER/PRIVATE-REPO:dev, name=api, PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=api_container.service, io.buildah.version=1.23.1)
Apr 22 11:09:46 api-sys podman[19623]: 2023-04-22 11:09:46.011344463 +0200 CEST m=+0.985662177 container died 7fe468112f2cd80a5f06ee6d5daea948975dfad30aaa0e16cfcfa4b99de2e13e (image=ghcr.io/USER/PRIVATE-REPO:dev, name=api)
Apr 22 11:09:46 api-sys podman[19623]: 2023-04-22 11:09:46.27965122 +0200 CEST m=+1.253968934 container cleanup 7fe468112f2cd80a5f06ee6d5daea948975dfad30aaa0e16cfcfa4b99de2e13e (image=ghcr.io/USER/PRIVATE-REPO:dev, name=api, io.buildah.version=1.23.1, PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=api_container.service)
Apr 22 11:09:46 api-sys podman[19623]: 7fe468112f2cd80a5f06ee6d5daea948975dfad30aaa0e16cfcfa4b99de2e13e
Apr 22 11:09:46 api-sys podman[19634]: 2023-04-22 11:09:46.288696728 +0200 CEST m=+0.366287529 container cleanup 7fe468112f2cd80a5f06ee6d5daea948975dfad30aaa0e16cfcfa4b99de2e13e (image=ghcr.io/USER/PRIVATE-REPO:dev, name=api, PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=api_container.service, io.buildah.version=1.23.1)
Apr 22 11:09:47 api-sys podman[19658]: 2023-04-22 11:09:47.023012748 +0200 CEST m=+0.426150060 container remove 7fe468112f2cd80a5f06ee6d5daea948975dfad30aaa0e16cfcfa4b99de2e13e (image=ghcr.io/USER/PRIVATE-REPO:dev, name=api, PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=api_container.service, io.buildah.version=1.23.1)
Apr 22 11:09:47 api-sys podman[19658]: 7fe468112f2cd80a5f06ee6d5daea948975dfad30aaa0e16cfcfa4b99de2e13e
Apr 22 11:09:47 api-sys systemd[524]: api_container.service: Succeeded.
Apr 22 11:09:47 api-sys systemd[524]: Stopped API Server (Podman Container).
Apr 22 11:09:47 api-sys systemd[524]: api_container.service: Consumed 4min 12.616s CPU time.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
It should stop the container gracefully because the process signal is handled properly, I think.

**Command outputs:**
I use the GitHub action `redhat-actions/buildah-build@v2` on a runner with `ubuntu-latest`. When running the built image, it says `io.buildah.version=1.23.1` in several places in the log.


---

I hope I am not misinterpreting the term "container **died**".
Can someone tell me what Buildah adds to the image?

comments.body: `io.buildah.version` is a label that buildah adds to an image at build-time unless the build is run with the `--identity-label=false` option.  Here it's just misleading because the image's labels appear to be included in the log.  The `container stop` message is an event being logged by podman, so I'll move the issue there.

Hi @MatsG23 , Looking at this https://github.com/MatsG23/buildah-issue I think you should use `podman kill` instead of `podman stop` , I think `podman kill` should send the single to your node server.

See: https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-kill.1.html

I think `podman kill` will solve your use-case hence closing this issue but please feel free to re-open.

I have tried the command `/usr/bin/podman kill --signal TERM --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id`. But I am getting issues, no matter if I use the argument `--signal TERM` or `--signal SIGTERM`. I do not want to kill the container using the `SIGKILL` signal.

The issue is not that the Node.JS process would not receive the process signal. I can see that because I am logging something in the handler for the `SIGTERM` signal. Is there some logic issue? I have showed the full code for the `SIGTERM` handler above. If I call `process.exit(0)` and exit the Node.JS process (works on my machine in the terminal), won't that stop the container itself? As I said, the Node.JS process has the PID 0.

Logs:
```
systemd[524]: Stopping API Server (Podman Container)...
podman[1067]: 2023-04-26 18:41:08.100540881 +0200 CEST m=+0.292161355 container kill bb91b03df221d2189ef0c957277bd131fd6eeee333789ed2d070ed207696912a (image=ghcr.io/OWNER/container-image:dev, n>
podman[1067]: bb91b03df221d2189ef0c957277bd131fd6eeee333789ed2d070ed207696912a
podman[631]: {"level":30,"time":1682527268124,"pid":1,"hostname":"bb91b03df221","msg":"Shutting down server and database pool..."}
podman[631]: 2023-04-26 18:41:08.192207868 +0200 CEST m=+159.542011522 container kill bb91b03df221d2189ef0c957277bd131fd6eeee333789ed2d070ed207696912a (image=ghcr.io/OWNER/container-image:dev, >
podman[631]: 2023-04-26 18:41:08.225816659 +0200 CEST m=+159.575620418 container kill bb91b03df221d2189ef0c957277bd131fd6eeee333789ed2d070ed207696912a (image=ghcr.io/OWNER/container-image:dev, >
podman[631]: 2023-04-26 18:41:08.254265703 +0200 CEST m=+159.604069305 container kill bb91b03df221d2189ef0c957277bd131fd6eeee333789ed2d070ed207696912a (image=ghcr.io/OWNER/container-image:dev, >
podman[631]: 2023-04-26 18:41:08.284663535 +0200 CEST m=+159.634467033 container kill bb91b03df221d2189ef0c957277bd131fd6eeee333789ed2d070ed207696912a (image=ghcr.io/OWNER/container-image:dev, >
podman[631]: qemu: uncaught target signal 11 (Segmentation fault) - core dumped
systemd[524]: api_container.service: State 'stop-sigterm' timed out. Killing.
systemd[524]: api_container.service: Killing process 1010 (conmon) with signal SIGKILL.
systemd[524]: api_container.service: Killing process 631 (podman) with signal SIGKILL.
systemd[524]: api_container.service: Killing process 644 (podman) with signal SIGKILL.
systemd[524]: api_container.service: Killing process 648 (podman) with signal SIGKILL.
systemd[524]: api_container.service: Killing process 649 (podman) with signal SIGKILL.
systemd[524]: api_container.service: Killing process 651 (podman) with signal SIGKILL.
systemd[524]: api_container.service: Killing process 658 (podman) with signal SIGKILL.
systemd[524]: api_container.service: Killing process 891 (n/a) with signal SIGKILL.
systemd[524]: api_container.service: Killing process 1012 (n/a) with signal SIGKILL.
systemd[524]: api_container.service: Main process exited, code=killed, status=9/KILL
podman[1096]: time="2023-04-26T18:42:18+02:00" level=error msg="error joining network namespace for container bb91b03df221d2189ef0c957277bd131fd6eeee333789ed2d070ed207696912a: error retrieving network namespace at /run/user/1000/netns/cni-c1a0ae3c-5382-6456-14fe-a72803c64bc0: unknown FS magic on \"/run/user/1000/netns/cni-c1a0ae3c-5382-6456-14fe-a72803c64bc0\": 1021994"
podman[1111]: send signal to pidfd: Operation not permitted
podman[1096]: panic: operation not permitted
podman[1096]: goroutine 15 [running]:
podman[1096]: github.com/containers/podman/libpod/lock.(*SHMLock).Unlock(0x40006e1450)
podman[1096]:         github.com/containers/podman/libpod/lock/shm_lock_manager_linux.go:121 +0x88
podman[1096]: github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Runtime).removeContainer(0x40005fc4e0, 0x19dcc60, 0x4000040098, 0x40005f57c0, 0x1, 0x19a0c00, 0x400059c0e0)
podman[1096]:         github.com/containers/podman/libpod/runtime_ctr.go:633 +0xd6c
podman[1096]: github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Runtime).RemoveContainer(0x40005fc4e0, 0x19dcc60, 0x4000040098, 0x40005f57c0, 0x1, 0x0, 0x0)
podman[1096]:         github.com/containers/podman/libpod/runtime_ctr.go:522 +0x98
podman[1096]: github.com/containers/podman/pkg/domain/infra/abi.(*ContainerEngine).ContainerRm.func1(0x40005f57c0, 0x0, 0x4000120f08)
podman[1096]:         github.com/containers/podman/pkg/domain/infra/abi/containers.go:303 +0x64
podman[1096]: github.com/containers/podman/pkg/parallel/ctr.ContainerOp.func1(0x4000644690, 0x19dcc60)
podman[1096]:         github.com/containers/podman/pkg/parallel/ctr/ctr.go:28 +0x30
podman[1096]: github.com/containers/podman/pkg/parallel.Enqueue.func1(0x400055e180, 0x19dcc60, 0x4000040098, 0x40002fe7a0)
podman[1096]:         github.com/containers/podman/pkg/parallel/parallel.go:66 +0x164
podman[1096]: created by github.com/containers/podman/pkg/parallel.Enqueue
podman[1096]:         github.com/containers/podman/pkg/parallel/parallel.go:55 +0x68
systemd[524]: api_container.service: Control process exited, code=exited, status=2/INVALIDARGUMENT
systemd[524]: api_container.service: Failed with result 'timeout'.
systemd[524]: Stopped API Server (Podman Container).
systemd[524]: api_container.service: Consumed 49.707s CPU time.
```
The actual issue here may be that the container itself does not stop (although it should). That the container is killed would be a side effect in that case.
